criteria: Longform (avg video >= 8 min), 3-4 total uploads, 100K+ total views, last upload within 3 weeks. Ranked by popularity (blend of avg views per video, outlier score, and consistency).

rankedChannels.title: Snowei

topVideo.title: A Day In Castle Town (Deltarune Fan Animation)

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Fan animation of cult-favourite games (Deltarune) drives massive replays within a passionate community. With only 4 uploads and a 61x outlier score the algorithm is already feeding the channel Browse impressions. Low script cost — story is pre-existing IP, visuals are hand-drawn animation.

rankedChannels.title: ScreenPuff

topVideo.title: When an audition changed TV forever

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Documentary-style deep-dives on iconic TV moments tap high curiosity-per-click. Highest raw avg views (521K) in the set with 32x outlier confirms strong algorithmic endorsement. Faceless execution via voiceover + archival clips makes this easy to replicate.

rankedChannels.title: katsulicious

topVideo.title: katseye random moments that will make you stan them part II

rankedChannels.whyWorking: K-pop fandom (Katseye) is one of the most loyal, high-rewatch communities on YouTube. A 51x outlier score on just 4 videos signals the algorithm is actively surfacing these. Niche is still small enough (6.2K subs) that a second channel in this space could capture significant share.

rankedChannels.title: Sergeant Mike

topVideo.title: What It’s Like To Be In Every Elite US Special Ops Unit

rankedChannels.whyWorking: US Special Forces content has perennial search demand and strong advertiser CPMs. 425K avg views on 3 uploads with minimal subscriber base confirms Browse is doing the heavy lifting. Animation format removes any production barrier — fully faceless and scalable.

rankedChannels.title: Potboiler

topVideo.title: The COMPLETE Pokémon Story SO FAR...

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Long-form Pokemon lore taps a nostalgia fanbase of hundreds of millions globally. 408K avg views on 3 uploads with 8.6x outlier; top video hit 1.15M views. Documentary-style research format is 100% faceless with no licensed footage risk.

rankedChannels.title: WINTHLETIC

topVideo.title: Thierry Henry Was Right About Mbappe

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Football/soccer analysis with a strong contrarian angle (Mbappe, Henry) generates debate and shares. 36x outlier on 4 videos with 3K subs shows it's finding audience well before subscriber base has scaled. Commentary-over-clips format is cheap and fast to produce.

rankedChannels.title: Ghosts of the Frontier

topVideo.title: Billy the Kid Tintype: The Shocking Truth Hidden in the Famous Photo

rankedChannels.whyWorking: American frontier history with true-crime mystery framing (Billy the Kid tintype) creates compulsive viewing. 25.6x outlier and $4.37 RPM means both algorithm and advertisers are responding well. Archival imagery + voiceover narration is a proven faceless formula.

rankedChannels.title: Levina

topVideo.title: I Got Pet Sea Ancient Monsters

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Gaming-mythology hybrid is an unusual combination that stands out in a crowded space. 13.8x outlier with 255K avg views shows the curiosity hook is working. Low production overhead — gameplay footage with narrated mythology overlay.

rankedChannels.title: MindTwist 40

topVideo.title: The African Eden: The Lie They Told The World

rankedChannels.whyWorking: Contrarian African history framing ('The Lie They Told The World') triggers high emotional engagement and sharing. Best RPM in the set at $5.10 with 16x outlier score despite 21K subs — still early. Educational documentary format is fully faceless.

rankedChannels.title: Photo Relic
rankedChannels.whyWorking: Rare archival logging and frontier photographs tap into a loyal history-buff audience. Highest outlier efficiency relative to sub count (2K subs, 20.6x outlier, 115K avg views). Zero footage cost — public domain images plus voiceover narration, very low production overhead.

patternSummary: Fan/fandom content (games, K-pop, Pokemon) is producing the highest raw view counts — established IP removes the hook-writing burden and delivers pre-loaded audiences.
History and military niches cluster at $4-5 RPM while animation and sports sit at $2, making history/military documentaries the best revenue-per-view play in this cohort.
All top 10 channels achieved exceptional view-to-subscriber ratios (avg 10:1+), confirming Browse Features as primary driver — not search or subscribers.
Channels with 3 uploads outperform 4-upload channels on avg views per video, suggesting the first three videos determine algorithmic fate before consistency dilutes performance.
Contrarian or mystery framing ('The Lie They Told', 'Shocking Truth', 'changed TV forever') appears in 6 of 10 top-video titles — curiosity/controversy gap is the dominant click driver.
